stimulus: In the 1970s, Yuri Kochiyama led a campaign in New York City to expand education programs for Asian American youth. Her work in this effort was inspired by her son, Billy Kochiyama. In 1969, Billy joined a series of community meetings organized by the Asian Americans for Action group in Lower Manhattan. Following her son, Yuri began attending these meetings too. Yuri soon became a member of the steering committee of Asian Americans for Action. She also became the head of what came to be called the Chinatown Youth Education Project.
stem: According to the text, what did Yuri Kochiyama lead?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer because the text states that Kochiyama "became the head of what came to be called the Chinatown Youth Education Project."

Choice A is incorrect; the text says her son inspired her, not that she led his school. Choice B is incorrect; the text does not mention any bookstore. Choice D is incorrect; the text describes education work, not environmental protests.
